Han Wang

About me

Yo! I’m Han Wang, an ECE & CS undergrad @ UW-Madison who’s figuring out his place in the world. I care a lot about my family/friends and always look for ways to spend more time with them, either chatting or going on foolish adventures together. I think a lot about tech and how it can make meaningful impacts on society

Right now, I am part of Simulation Based Engineering Lab (SBEL) at UW-Madison. At SBEL, I help develop sensor simulation for autonomous vehicles in Project Chrono. Project Chrono is an open source physics based simulation infrastructure implemented in C++.

I love reading, especially biographies and history. Every book teachs me something new or helps me see things differently. I was lucky to have parents who encouraged me to read. Reading fuels a sense of curiousity about the world, which I think helped drive me forward to a lot of what I do.

Nerd Stuff

GPU Computing for Beginners

The Importance of Simulation

What is Realism?

Books I Read

Grand Strategy by John Gaddis

Becoming by Melinda Gates

Edcuated by Tara Westover

Evicted by Matthew Desmond

The Emperor of All Maladies by Siddhartha Mukherjee

When Breath Becomes Air by Paul Kalanithi

Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind

The World Until Yesterday by Jared Diamond

Guns, Germs, and Steel by Jared Diamond

Man's Search for Meaning by Viktor Frankl
